title: Allantodia nipponica
taxonomy: Allantodia nipponica is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Allantodia. The genus Allantodia is a part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various species of ferns. This species is native to East Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns in the same family and order. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta.

intro: Allantodia nipponica, also known as Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Allantodia nipponica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
